x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces the Manhattan DA's newsroom (the one machine-readable object, via the WP REST API) plus design-first contracts for its bureaus/initiatives, victim resources, offices, and aggregate prosecution reporting, and adds submit_tip — the net-new write workflow that today has no API (only a reCAPTCHA-gated contact form; FOIL is delegated to NYC OpenRecords).

server.name: io.nyc.manhattanda
server.title: Manhattan District Attorney's Office
server.description: Agent-native access to the Manhattan District Attorney's Office (New York County District Attorney) — the newsroom (press releases, media coverage, op-eds, reports), the office's bureaus/units/initiatives (Our Work, including diversion and conviction integrity), victim and community resources, office locations, and aggregate prosecution statistics, plus the net-new ability to submit and track a tip to a DA bureau. The office publishes no NYC Open Data; only the newsroom is machine-readable today (WordPress REST API).
server.instructions: Use find_press_releases / get_press_release for the newsroom — the only genuinely machine-readable content (WP REST API, ~3,029 posts). find_programs / get_program cover the office's bureaus, units, and initiatives (Our Work), including diversion and post-conviction / conviction-integrity work. find_victim_services returns victim and community resources by crime type. find_prosecution_statistics returns AGGREGATE caseload figures only — the office never exposes an individual case or defendant, and there is no NYC Open Data twin, so treat these as design-first. find_offices lists the Main Office (One Hogan Place) and community offices. submit_tip (net-new) reports suspected wrongdoing to a bureau; it mutates state — confirm the category, subject, and description with the user first, and note that anonymous tips cannot receive status updates. FOIL records requests are NOT handled here; direct users to NYC OpenRecords.

tools.name: find_press_releases
tools.title: Find Press Releases
tools.description: Search the Manhattan DA newsroom (press releases, media coverage, op-eds, reports, testimony, remarks, video) by type, topic, date range, or free text.

tools.name: get_press_release
tools.title: Get Press Release
tools.description: Fetch a single newsroom item (headline, date, body, topics) by its WordPress post ID.

tools.name: find_programs
tools.title: Find Programs
tools.description: List the office's bureaus, units, and initiatives (Our Work) by type, crime category, or diversion flag — including gun violence, hate crimes, white-collar crime, community partnerships, reform & fairness, and post-conviction / conviction integrity.

tools.name: get_program
tools.title: Get Program
tools.description: Fetch a bureau, unit, or initiative by its slug under /our-work/.

tools.name: find_victim_services
tools.title: Find Victim Services
tools.description: List victim and community resources by crime type (child abuse, elder abuse, financial crimes, human trafficking, intimate partner & sexual violence, hate crimes, housing/tenant protection, worker protection / wage theft) or language.

tools.name: find_prosecution_statistics
tools.title: Find Prosecution Statistics
tools.description: Return AGGREGATE caseload / prosecution figures (arraignments, indictments, dispositions, convictions, declinations, diversions) by reporting period, bureau, category, or charge level. Design-first: no NYC Open Data twin, and never an individual case.

tools.name: find_offices
tools.title: Find Offices
tools.description: List Manhattan DA office locations (Main Office at One Hogan Place plus community offices in Harlem and Washington Heights) with addresses and phone numbers.

tools.name: submit_tip
tools.title: Submit a Tip
tools.description: Submit a tip to a Manhattan DA bureau (report suspected financial fraud, elder abuse, human trafficking, a hate crime, public corruption, cybercrime, and more). NET-NEW workflow with no public API today — the site offers only a generic reCAPTCHA-gated contact form. Mutates state — confirm category, subject, and description first; set anonymous=true to submit without contact details (no status updates possible). FOIL records requests are NOT handled here (they go to NYC OpenRecords).

tools.name: get_tip
tools.title: Get Tip Status
tools.description: Fetch a previously submitted tip and its intake status by reference number. Requires the submitter to be authenticated; anonymous tips are not retrievable.

x-agent-skills.note: Each tool maps to one of the ten NYC common government-process Agent Skills via x-agent-skill.

prompts.name: explore_manhattanda
prompts.description: Search and read Manhattan District Attorney records — Newsroom, Programs, VictimServices, Prosecution.

prompts.name: report_manhattanda
prompts.description: TipSubmission — submit a routable tip to a DA bureau (no API today; only a generic reCAPTCHA contact form). FOIL is delegated off-site to NYC OpenRecords.

prompts.name: check_manhattanda_status
prompts.description: Check the status of a Manhattan District Attorney application, case, or request.

resources.name: Manhattan District Attorney — profile
resources.description: Manhattan District Attorney: 10 operations, 9 tools, verb “standardize”.

resources.name: Newsroom record (templated)
resources.description: A single Manhattan District Attorney Newsroom record by id.
